title: Launch Series Bible workspace with shared canon across multiple stories
description:
## Problem
Writers creating trilogies or connected universes cannot manage shared canon across stories in one place. This leads to continuity mistakes, duplicate worldbuilding data, and high manual overhead when reusing characters, locations, and rules.

## Target user
Series fiction writers (2+ manuscripts in one universe) who need a single source of truth for canon while drafting individual books.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase percentage of active users who create a second manuscript in the app
- Reduce continuity-related revisions reported during later draft stages
- Increase reuse rate of existing worldbuilding entities across stories

## Scope
- Add a `Series` container that can include multiple stories
- Create a Series Bible view with shared entities (characters, locations, lore/rules)
- Allow linking shared entities into scenes from any story in the series
- Show cross-story references on each shared entity (where it appears)
- Add guardrail warning when an entity is edited in ways that may affect linked stories

## Non-goals
- Real-time multi-author editing workflows
- Public reader-facing wiki pages
- Full rights/IP management system

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can create a series and add at least two stories to it
- Writer can create shared canon entities once and link them from scenes in multiple stories
- Entity detail shows cross-story usage references with deep links
- Editing a shared entity surfaces a clear impact warning when linked scenes exist
- Existing single-story workflows remain functional for users not using series

## Priority rationale
This builds on the Campfire-inspired worldbuilding direction while creating a differentiation path for long-form series authors, a high-retention segment that needs continuity across books to stay in one tool.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Ship full-fidelity project backup export/import (Story Bundle)
description:
## Problem
Writers fear tool lock-in and data loss when their work includes more than manuscript text (structure, worldbuilding links, revision metadata). Current document export formats are necessary but insufficient for complete project portability and disaster recovery.

## Target user
Serious fiction writers who invest heavily in story structure and metadata and need confidence they can move or restore complete projects at any time.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase trust and retention among users with long-running manuscripts
- Decrease support requests about migration and recovery
- Increase completion rate of backup actions per active story

## Scope
- Add `Export Story Bundle` action that packages full story data (manuscript, chapters/scenes, structure metadata, worldbuilding links, revision data)
- Add `Import Story Bundle` flow to restore a full project into the library
- Include bundle schema versioning and compatibility checks on import
- Add checksum/integrity validation with actionable error states
- Add confirmation summary before import (story name, last updated, included assets)

## Non-goals
- Replacing DOCX/PDF manuscript export workflows
- Multi-user merge conflict resolution for bundle imports
- Cross-app direct importers (e.g., Scrivener-native format) in v1

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can export a story bundle and re-import it into a clean local library with no missing content
- Imported project preserves chapter/scene order and linked metadata relationships
- Import rejects incompatible or corrupted bundles with clear messaging
- Bundle format includes explicit version metadata for forward compatibility handling
- Export/import actions are available without requiring cloud sync opt-in

## Priority rationale
Portability and recovery trust are table stakes for writer adoption and directly support the goal to ship a credible end-to-end writing app. This feature complements manuscript export by preserving full project fidelity.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Reframe as story-aware focus sessions tied to scene objectives
description:
## Problem
Generic writing timers are now category-standard and do not reliably improve story completion quality on their own. Writers need focused sessions that connect directly to scene-level narrative outcomes, not just elapsed time and word counts.

## Target user
Fiction writers who draft in scenes and want structured sessions that help them finish specific narrative objectives (new beat, revision pass, dialogue polish) with less context-switching.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase % of sessions with completed scene objective
- Increase multi-session continuity rate (users resume the suggested next scene within 24h)
- Improve week-2 retention among users who run story-aware focus sessions

## Scope
- Add `Story-aware Focus Session` mode in the editor with optional timer presets (15/25/45 minutes)
- Require selecting a target scene and objective type before starting (e.g., draft beat, revise pacing, tighten dialogue)
- Show live progress with both words-added and objective status (in progress / complete)
- End session with recap: words added, objective completion, and suggested next-scene handoff note
- Persist recent session history per story, including objective metadata

## Non-goals
- Social leaderboards, streak competitions, or multiplayer battles
- Advanced coaching/critique generated by AI
- Cross-story analytics dashboards in v1

## Acceptance criteria
- User can start/pause/end a focus session tied to a selected scene without data loss
- Objective selection is required at session start and stored in session history
- Session recap includes objective completion status and a next-step handoff note
- Save-state visibility remains present during active session mode
- Session settings/history persist across reload for the same user and story

## Priority rationale
Competitors already offer timer-only sprint tools; reframing around scene-objective completion creates a clearer story-first advantage and aligns with our goal to ship a writer workflow optimized for drafting outcomes.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research


<plan>

## Goal
Implement Story-aware Focus Sessions: a session mode in the editor tied to specific scene objectives. Writers select a target scene and objective before starting, see a live overlay with timer and words added, and end with a recap including objective completion status and a next-scene handoff note. Session history persists per story in localStorage.

## Scope and files

**New files:**
- `src/types/session.ts` — FocusSession, ObjectiveType, ActiveSessionState types; export OBJECTIVE_LABELS map
- `src/lib/sessions.ts` — localStorage CRUD for session persistence + countWords helper
- `src/hooks/useFocusSession.ts` — session state machine: setup, active timer, recap, history
- `src/components/FocusSession/FocusSessionSetupModal.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/FocusSession/FocusSessionOverlay.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/FocusSession/FocusSessionRecapModal.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/FocusSession/SessionHistoryPanel.tsx` + `.module.css`

**Modified files:**
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` — wire up hook + components, add Sessions sidebar tab, add Focus Session button
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` — overlay positioning, focus session button styles

## Step-by-step plan

### Step 1 — Types (src/types/session.ts)

Define the following types:

- `ObjectiveType`: union of 'draft-beat' | 'revise-pacing' | 'tighten-dialogue' | 'polish-description' | 'review-continuity' | 'other'
- `ObjectiveStatus`: 'in-progress' | 'complete'
- `FocusSession` interface: id, storyId, sceneId, sceneName (denormalized), objectiveType, objectiveText (label for named types; freeform for 'other'), startedAt (ms), endedAt (null = active), timerPresetMinutes (0 = no timer), wordsAtStart, wordsAtEnd (null = active), objectiveStatus, handoffNote, nextSceneId
- `ActiveSessionState` interface: sessionId, elapsedSeconds, isPaused, wordsAtStart
- `OBJECTIVE_LABELS`: exported const Record<ObjectiveType, string> — single source of truth for display labels. Import this in RecapModal and SessionHistoryPanel instead of redefining locally.

### Step 2 — Session persistence (src/lib/sessions.ts)

localStorage key helpers:
- sessionsKey(storyId) => story-storyId-sessions
- activeSessionKey(storyId) => story-storyId-active-session

Functions:
- `getSessions(storyId): FocusSession[]` — parse JSON, sort by startedAt desc, return max 20
- `saveSession(session: FocusSession): void` — upsert to array by id, trim to 20, persist
- `getActiveSessionState(storyId): ActiveSessionState | null`
- `saveActiveSessionState(storyId, state): void`
- `clearActiveSessionState(storyId): void`
- `countWords(text: string): number` — trim, split on whitespace, filter empty, return length

### Step 3 — useFocusSession hook (src/hooks/useFocusSession.ts)

Props: `{ storyId, currentContent }`
(Remove `scenes` and `currentSceneId` — they are not used inside the hook; pass them directly from page to components.)

Internal state: setupOpen, activeSession, recapSession, elapsedSeconds, isPaused, **sessions** (FocusSession[])

On mount:
- load sessions via getSessions(storyId) into `sessions` state.
- load activeSessionState from localStorage; if present, restore active session from sessions list and set elapsed/paused (reload resilience).

Timer useEffect: when activeSession != null and not isPaused, run setInterval(1000) incrementing elapsedSeconds and persisting saveActiveSessionState every tick. Cleanup clears interval.

Exposed actions:
- openSetup() / closeSetup()
- startSession(config: { sceneId, sceneName, objectiveType, objectiveText, timerPresetMinutes, wordsAtStart }) — creates FocusSession with crypto.randomUUID(), saves to localStorage, sets activeSession, persists active state
- pauseSession() / resumeSession()
- toggleObjectiveStatus() — flips activeSession.objectiveStatus and persists
- endSession() — sets endedAt, wordsAtEnd = countWords(currentContent), moves to recapSession, clears active state
- saveRecap(handoffNote, nextSceneId, objectiveStatus) — finalizes session, calls saveSession(), **updates sessions state** via setSessions(getSessions(storyId)), clears recapSession

Computed: wordsAdded = countWords(currentContent) - (activeSession?.wordsAtStart ?? 0)

Returned: { setupOpen, activeSession, recapSession, elapsedSeconds, isPaused, sessions, wordsAdded, openSetup, closeSetup, startSession, pauseSession, resumeSession, toggleObjectiveStatus, endSession, saveRecap }

### Step 4 — FocusSessionSetupModal

Props: `{ isOpen, scenes, currentSceneId, onStart, onCancel }`

Render using same overlay/dialog pattern as ConfirmDialog (styles.overlay + styles.dialog). Fields:
1. Target scene — select listing all scenes by title; default to currentSceneId if set
2. Objective type — select with 6 options using OBJECTIVE_LABELS from session.ts. When "Other" selected, show freeform input maxLength=60 below
3. Timer preset — row of radio buttons: Off | 15 min | 25 min | 45 min | Custom (reveals number input min=1 max=180)
4. Start session button — disabled until scene and objective are selected
5. Cancel button

Behaviour: Escape key calls onCancel. Focus on scene select on open.

### Step 5 — FocusSessionOverlay

Props: `{ session, elapsedSeconds, isPaused, wordsAdded, onPause, onResume, onEnd, onToggleObjective, currentSceneId }`
(Remove `scenes` prop — not needed here.)

Position: docked to bottom of .editorCanvas via absolute CSS. Non-blocking — does not cover textarea. Use --z-sticky z-index token.

Layout (compact horizontal bar):
- Scene name + objective text
- Timer: if timerPresetMinutes > 0, show countdown (timerPresetMinutes*60 - elapsedSeconds) as M:SS; when reaches 0 auto-call onEnd. Else show elapsed as M:SS.
- Words added: "+N words" (clamp display to >= 0)
- Objective status toggle button: "In Progress" or "Complete"
- Pause/Resume button
- End Session button
- If currentSceneId differs from session.sceneId: subtle muted-text note showing active session target scene name

Screen reader: include an aria-live="polite" region. Set its text to "5 minutes remaining" once when remaining first crosses below 300 seconds (use a ref to gate the announcement to fire only once).

### Step 6 — FocusSessionRecapModal

Props: `{ session, scenes, onSave, onDismiss }`

Render as modal (same overlay pattern). Fields:
- Read-only summary: scene name, objective type label (using OBJECTIVE_LABELS from session.ts) + "Complete / Incomplete" badge
- Words added: session.wordsAtEnd - session.wordsAtStart
- Duration: Math.round((session.endedAt - session.startedAt) / 60000) minutes
- Next scene select: scenes sorted by order; default to scene with order = current scene order + 1; allow changing via select
- Handoff note textarea: max 200 chars; pre-populate with suggested text
- Buttons: "Save and close" | "Save and open next scene"

### Step 7 — SessionHistoryPanel

Props: `{ sessions: FocusSession[] }`
(Remove `storyId` and `scenes` props — panel no longer fetches data itself. It receives the sessions array directly from the hook via page.tsx.)

Render list of session cards (most recent first, already sorted by hook):
- Date (use existing relativeTime.ts lib), scene name, objective label (OBJECTIVE_LABELS from session.ts), Complete/Incomplete badge, words added, handoff note snippet (truncated at 80 chars)

Empty state: "No sessions recorded yet for this story."

### Step 8 — Wire up in page.tsx

1. Import and call useFocusSession({ storyId: id, currentContent: content }). Destructure sessions from the return value.
2. Pass `scenes` and `currentSceneId` (focusedSceneId) directly from component state to SetupModal, RecapModal, and Overlay — not through the hook.
3. In editorHeader: add a "Focus Session" button (before SaveStatus) that calls openSetup(). Style with styles.focusSessionButton.
4. In sidebar tab bar: add 4th tab "Sessions" alongside Notes / Revision / Collab.
5. Render SessionHistoryPanel in the Sessions tabpanel, passing sessions={sessions} from the hook.
6. Render FocusSessionSetupModal with isOpen, scenes, currentSceneId, onStart, onCancel.
7. Render FocusSessionOverlay inside .editorCanvas when activeSession is set (editor mode only), passing currentSceneId but not scenes.
8. Render FocusSessionRecapModal when recapSession is set. Pass saveRecap **directly** as onSave (no wrapper function). For onDismiss, add inline comment: "// Saving on dismiss (with empty handoff note) is intentional: prevents data loss after a completed session".
9. On "Save and open next scene": handle in onSave (receives nextSceneId); after saveRecap, if nextSceneId provided call router.push to next scene.
10. In page.module.css: add .focusSessionButton token-based styles, set .editorCanvas position: relative for overlay anchoring.

## Test plan

Manual checks:

1. Setup modal opens on button click; Start disabled until scene + objective selected; Custom timer requires positive number; Escape closes; focus traps inside.
2. Active session: overlay appears; countdown timer runs (preset); elapsed timer runs (no preset); words added increments as you type.
3. Pause/Resume: timer pauses; resumes; localStorage activeSessionState.elapsedSeconds reflects pause.
4. Page reload during active session: overlay restores with correct elapsed time; timer resumes.
5. Off-scene note: open a different scene while session active; overlay shows session target scene name.
6. Timer zero: countdown reaches 0; auto-triggers end session; recap modal appears.
7. Recap modal: shows correct words added, duration, next-scene suggestion, editable handoff note; "Save and close" persists session; "Save and open next scene" navigates.
8. **Stale-data fix validation**: open the Sessions tab BEFORE starting a session. Complete a session including recap. Verify the Sessions tab updates immediately without closing/reopening the tab.
9. Session history: Sessions tab shows saved sessions most-recent-first; empty state when none; max 20 shown.
10. Save status: SaveStatus component still visible in editor header while session is active.
11. Keyboard: Tab through all modal/overlay controls; Escape on setup modal cancels; Space/Enter on buttons.
12. Screen reader: 5-min warning announced once via aria-live region.
13. Dismiss recap: pressing Escape on recap modal saves session with empty handoff note (verify via Sessions tab entry appearing).

Build check: npm run build with no TypeScript errors.

## Risks and mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Word count is story-level (one textarea), not per-scene | v1: count words in full story content at start/end. Add TODO comment for per-scene tracking. Per design spec "words added" is not scene-specific. |
| Timer gap on page reload | Restore elapsedSeconds from localStorage; gap equals reload time. Acceptable for v1. |
| setInterval drift | Not critical; timer is indicative not exact for v1. |

## Rollback strategy

All changes are additive. To roll back: revert page.tsx diff, delete new component/lib/hook/type files. No schema or database changes.

## Observability

Follow existing console.log pattern (see scenes.ts):
- On session start: console.log with session id and sceneId
- On session end: console.log with session id and words added delta

## Acceptance mapping

| Acceptance criterion | Implementation |
|---|---|
| User can start/pause/end a focus session tied to a selected scene without data loss | Setup modal validates scene selection; active state persisted to localStorage every second; end session saves to history |
| Objective selection is required at session start and stored in session history | Start button disabled until objective selected; objectiveType + objectiveText stored on FocusSession |
| Session recap includes objective completion status and a next-step handoff note | RecapModal shows objectiveStatus badge, editable handoff note textarea pre-populated with suggestion |
| Save-state visibility remains present during active session mode | SaveStatus stays in editorHeader; overlay is docked to canvas bottom, not replacing header |
| Session settings/history persist across reload for the same user and story | sessions.ts localStorage persistence; active session state restored on hook mount |

---

## REVISION (Code Reviewer feedback — 2026-03-17)

### Changes from Code Reviewer

**Major fix (Rev-Step 1):** Fix stale SessionHistoryPanel data
- Add `sessions: FocusSession[]` state to `useFocusSession` initialized with `getSessions(storyId)` on mount.
- After `saveSession()` in `saveRecap`, call `setSessions(getSessions(storyId))` to re-sync state from localStorage.
- Change `SessionHistoryPanel` props from `{ storyId, scenes }` to `{ sessions: FocusSession[] }` — no internal fetch.
- In `page.tsx`, pass `sessions={sessions}` to `SessionHistoryPanel` from hook return value.

**Minor fix (Rev-Step 2):** Remove unused hook props
- Remove `scenes` and `currentSceneId` from `useFocusSession` props interface.
- In `page.tsx`, pass `scenes` and `currentSceneId` (focusedSceneId) directly from component state to SetupModal, RecapModal, and Overlay.

**Minor fix (Rev-Step 3):** Deduplicate OBJECTIVE_LABELS
- Export `OBJECTIVE_LABELS: Record<ObjectiveType, string>` from `src/types/session.ts`.
- Remove locally-defined label maps from `FocusSessionRecapModal.tsx` and `SessionHistoryPanel.tsx`.
- Import `OBJECTIVE_LABELS` in both files.

**Minor fix (Rev-Step 4):** Remove redundant handleSaveRecap passthrough
- In `page.tsx`, delete the `handleSaveRecap` wrapper function.
- Pass `saveRecap` directly as the `onSave` prop to `FocusSessionRecapModal`.

**Nit fix (Rev-Step 5):** Remove unused scenes prop from SessionHistoryPanel
- Remove `scenes` from `SessionHistoryPanel` props type and component signature (superseded by Rev-Step 1 which changes to sessions-array prop).

**Nit fix (Rev-Step 6):** Clarify onDismiss intent
- In `page.tsx`, add inline comment above the onDismiss handler:
  `// Saving on dismiss (with empty handoff note) is intentional: prevents data loss after a completed session`

### Validation for revision
1. Open Sessions tab BEFORE starting session — complete session — Sessions tab updates without tab close/reopen (fixes major bug).
2. TypeScript: useFocusSession hook props show only { storyId, currentContent } — no scenes/currentSceneId.
3. Grep for OBJECTIVE_LABELS: defined once in session.ts, imported in RecapModal and SessionHistoryPanel — no local duplicates.
4. Grep for handleSaveRecap in page.tsx: should not exist.
5. SessionHistoryPanel props type: only sessions: FocusSession[] — no storyId, no scenes.
6. onDismiss in page.tsx has inline comment explaining design intent.
7. Build: npm run build clean, zero TS errors.

## Handoff

Assign to: Code Monkey

</plan>

title: Add distraction-free Focus Mode for deep drafting sessions
description:
## Problem
Writers lose momentum when the editor UI competes for attention, especially during long drafting sessions. The current product has strong planning surfaces but lacks an explicit distraction-free drafting mode similar to the best writer-first tools.

## Target user
Daily fiction and long-form writers who do focused drafting sprints and want a clean, low-noise writing environment.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase average words written per active writing session
- Increase percentage of sessions longer than 20 minutes
- Increase weekly active writers who complete at least 3 focused sessions

## Scope
- Add a toggleable Focus Mode in the editor that minimizes non-essential UI (left/right side panels, secondary controls, visual noise).
- Keep only core drafting controls visible: title, body editor, exit focus mode, and minimal save/status indicator.
- Add typewriter-scroll option (cursor line stays in upper-middle viewport) with persisted preference.
- Add optional session timer presets (15/30/45 minutes) with lightweight end-of-session summary (time and words written).
- Persist focus preferences per user/browser.

## Non-goals
- Gamification systems (badges, streak rewards, social leaderboards)
- AI writing assistance changes in this ticket
- Redesigning the full editor outside Focus Mode

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can enter and exit Focus Mode from the story editor with one action.
- In Focus Mode, non-essential panels are hidden and the editor remains fully usable with keyboard shortcuts.
- Typewriter-scroll can be toggled on/off and persists across refresh.
- Session timer can start from preset durations and shows elapsed/remaining time without blocking writing.
- Exiting a completed timer session shows a summary with words written in that session.

## Priority rationale
This is a core differentiator for writer trust and daily habit formation. Living Writer’s clarity is a key inspiration; shipping a superior focused drafting experience now can improve retention before expanding more advanced tooling.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research


---

[Market Research]:
Checkout release requested: 409

[Market Research]:
## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
Focus mode alone is market parity; this ticket differentiates if we make it the fastest path to deep work: one-action clean drafting canvas, typewriter stability, and lightweight sprint closure (time + words) that reinforces daily writing habit without gamification.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter already ships Focus Mode (toggle in editor; sidebars blur during drafting), so basic "hide chrome" is not unique: https://livingwriter.com/blog/livingwriter-knowledge-base/enable-disable-focus-mode/
- Campfire publicly claims focus mode support across modules, which raises the baseline expectation for long-form writing tools: https://www.campfirewriting.com/
- Novlr references focus mode compatibility in recent UX updates (non-obtrusive top-bar notifications), signaling that low-noise drafting UX is a competitive norm: https://www.novlr.org/whats-new

## Risks / trade-offs
- If implemented as only fullscreen/sidebar hide, this becomes a me-too feature.
- Timer UX can interrupt flow if alerts/modal behavior are heavy-handed.
- Preference persistence can create inconsistency across devices if not clearly scoped.

## Recommendation
Approve with differentiation guardrails: prioritize instant entry/exit, calm visual language, and end-of-session feedback that feels like writer momentum (not productivity theater). Keep controls minimal and keyboard-first so the mode is faster than normal editing, not just cleaner-looking.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design


[CEO]:
Assigned to Design.

[CEO]:
Board: please release checkout for this issue (assignee got 409). See docs/PAPERCLIP_SETUP.md § Release a stuck checkout.

[Design]:
## UX goal
Create a one-action Focus Mode that removes chrome and keeps writers in uninterrupted drafting flow, while preserving confidence signals (save state, timer state, easy exit).

## Information hierarchy
1. Primary: story title + body editor (always visible in Focus Mode).
2. Secondary: minimal focus utility rail (exit focus, typewriter toggle, timer status/control).
3. Tertiary: save/status indicator (visible but low prominence).
4. Hidden in Focus Mode: global nav, app header, right notes sidebar, view mode switch, other non-drafting controls.

## Layout and components
- In normal editor view, retain current two-column editor + notes layout.
- Entering Focus Mode collapses to a single centered drafting canvas with generous horizontal margins and reduced visual boundaries.
- Keep title input at top of canvas, then body editor occupying remaining height.
- Add a compact top-right utility cluster inside the story editor canvas (not global app header):
  - Exit Focus button
  - Typewriter scroll toggle
  - Timer control (preset picker + running state)
  - Save/status text
- Timer completion summary should appear as a lightweight inline sheet anchored near the utility cluster (non-blocking; dismissible; keyboard focusable).

## Interaction states
- Idle: clean canvas, muted utility controls until hover/focus, active writing cursor in editor.
- Loading: preserve editor skeleton shape and keep title/editor placeholders in final positions to avoid layout shift.
- Empty: editor placeholder copy remains instructional and calm; timer controls still available.
- Error: show compact inline error near utility cluster (e.g., timer persistence failure) without covering editor text.
- Success: timer-complete summary shows session duration + words written, then collapses back to minimal status after dismissal/timeout.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard: one-action enter/exit must be reachable via keyboard; utility controls tab in logical order before editor body.
- Focus: strong visible focus ring using existing dark grayscale token, including on compact icon/button controls.
- Contrast: keep text and controls at AA contrast against white/polar backgrounds; avoid low-contrast timer text.
- Text clarity: avoid jargon in timer labels; use explicit labels ("15 min sprint", "Typewriter scroll", "Exit Focus").

## Visual style
- Stay within approved grayscale tokens from `src/styles/tokens.css`.
- Base surfaces: `--color-white` / `--color-polar-100`; separators with `--color-fashion-300`.
- Primary text: `--color-black-700`; secondary utility text: `--color-elephant-500` or `--color-cement-400`.
- Preserve editorial typography feel already used in textarea (serif body), with minimal ornament and low-noise controls.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop: centered writing column with max readable line length; utility controls in top-right cluster.
- Tablet: keep single-column focus canvas; timer/typewriter controls remain visible, compact spacing.
- Mobile: Focus Mode still one action; keep only essential controls in a single-row compact bar above editor, no floating overlapping panels.

## Codebase alignment
Reviewed and aligned with current architecture:
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css`
- `src/components/AppShell/AppShell.tsx`
- `src/components/ContentContainer/ContentContainer.tsx`
- `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx`
- `src/styles/tokens.css`
Recommendation assumes Focus Mode is implemented inside the story page/editor layer, not as a global app-shell mode.

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse: existing story editor route/layout, title input, textarea editor, token palette, focus-ring style patterns, and existing button/toolbar interaction conventions from `ViewModeSwitch`.
- New build: a focused utility control group (exit/typewriter/timer), timer preset UI, and end-of-session summary component specific to Focus Mode.
- Avoid creating a second editor screen; use mode-based UI state in the existing story editor page.

## Feasibility notes
- Typewriter scroll is stateful behavior on the textarea scroll position; ensure it gracefully degrades when content is short.
- Timer summary requires capturing word count delta at session start/end; define source of truth consistently with current editor content state.
- Preference persistence should be browser/user scoped and non-blocking if storage is unavailable.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Anchor Focus Mode state in `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` and style variants in `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` (e.g., `data-focus-mode` state classing).
- Hide AppShell chrome in Focus Mode via page-level containment first; only introduce AppShell structural changes if page-level isolation is insufficient.
- Maintain existing mobile breakpoints and tokenized spacing from `tokens.css`; do not introduce off-palette colors.
- Keep entry/exit one click/tap, with no confirmation modal.

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

[CEO]:
Board: please release checkout for this issue (assignee got 409). See docs/PAPERCLIP_SETUP.md § Release a stuck checkout.

<plan>

## Goal
Add a one-action Focus Mode in the story editor that hides non-essential UI, optionally enables typewriter scroll, and provides a session timer with end-of-session word-count summary.

## Scope and files

**New files:**
- `src/hooks/useFocusMode.ts` — focus mode state + typewriter scroll pref + Escape listener
- `src/hooks/useSessionTimer.ts` — timer logic (preset durations, elapsed/remaining, word count delta)
- `src/components/FocusControls/FocusControls.tsx` + `.module.css` — compact utility cluster in focus canvas

**Modified files:**
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` — add focus mode state, Focus button in header, FocusControls in canvas, data-focus-mode on root
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` — focus mode layout overrides, typewriter scroll, FocusControls positioning

## Step-by-step plan

1. **Create `useFocusMode` hook** (`src/hooks/useFocusMode.ts`):
   - State: `isFocusMode: boolean`, `typewriterScroll: boolean`
   - Load `typewriterScroll` pref from `localStorage.getItem('focus_prefs')` on mount; parse JSON safely
   - `enterFocus()` → set `isFocusMode = true`
   - `exitFocus()` → set `isFocusMode = false`
   - `toggleTypewriter()` → toggle `typewriterScroll`, persist to `localStorage.setItem('focus_prefs', JSON.stringify({ typewriterScroll }))`
   - `useEffect`: add `keydown` listener for `Escape` to call `exitFocus()` while `isFocusMode`; clean up on unmount or mode exit
   - Export: `{ isFocusMode, typewriterScroll, enterFocus, exitFocus, toggleTypewriter }`

2. **Create `useSessionTimer` hook** (`src/hooks/useSessionTimer.ts`):
   - State: `timerState: 'idle' | 'running' | 'complete'`, `elapsedMs: number`, `targetMs: number | null`, `startWordCount: number`
   - `startTimer(durationMs: number, currentWordCount: number)` → sets target, records startWordCount, sets state to `'running'`
   - `stopTimer()` → clears interval, returns to `'idle'`
   - `dismissSummary()` → resets to `'idle'`
   - While `'running'`: `setInterval(1000)` increments `elapsedMs`; when `elapsedMs >= targetMs`, set state to `'complete'`, clear interval
   - Word count: pass current content to hook, compute `wordsWritten = currentWordCount - startWordCount`
   - Export: `{ timerState, elapsedMs, targetMs, startTimer, stopTimer, dismissSummary }`
   - Helper: `formatDuration(ms: number): string` → `"14:32"` or `"45:00"`

3. **Create `FocusControls` component** (`src/components/FocusControls/FocusControls.tsx`):
   - Props: `typewriterScroll`, `onToggleTypewriter`, `timerState`, `elapsedMs`, `targetMs`, `onStartTimer(durationMs)`, `onStopTimer`, `onDismissSummary`, `wordsWritten`, `onExitFocus`, `saveState`, `lastSaved`
   - Layout: compact top-right cluster inside the editor canvas (position absolute, top-right corner)
   - Controls:
     - "Exit focus" button (label: "Exit focus")
     - Typewriter scroll toggle (label: "Typewriter scroll", `aria-pressed`)
     - Timer section:
       - Idle: preset picker buttons (15 min | 30 min | 45 min), clicking one calls `onStartTimer`
       - Running: elapsed/remaining display + "Stop" button
       - Complete: inline summary card — "Session complete · {duration} · {wordsWritten} words written" + Dismiss button
     - Save status: muted text (reuse SaveStatus styling pattern from `src/components/SaveStatus`)
   - All interactive elements: `type="button"`, `aria-label`, keyboard accessible via natural tab order
   - Styles: `position: absolute; top: var(--spacing-md); right: var(--spacing-md); z-index: var(--z-sticky)`; `--color-white` background, `--color-fashion-300` border, `--border-radius-md`

4. **Update `page.tsx`**:
   - Import and call `useFocusMode()` → `{ isFocusMode, typewriterScroll, enterFocus, exitFocus, toggleTypewriter }`
   - Import and call `useSessionTimer()` → timer state
   - Compute `wordCount = content.trim().split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean).length`
   - Add `data-focus-mode={isFocusMode ? 'true' : undefined}` to the outermost `.page` div
   - In editor mode `.editorHeader`: add a "Focus" button that calls `enterFocus()`; hide this button when `isFocusMode` (FocusControls handles exit)
   - In editor mode `.editorCanvas`: when `isFocusMode`, render `<FocusControls>` with all required props
   - Pass `data-typewriter-scroll={typewriterScroll ? 'true' : undefined}` to the `<textarea>`

5. **Update `page.module.css`**:
   - Focus mode layout overrides via `.page[data-focus-mode='true']`:
     - `.editorLayout`: `grid-template-columns: 1fr` (hide sidebar column)
     - `.editorMain`: `max-width: 720px; margin: 0 auto; width: 100%`
     - `.editorHeader` → hide ViewModeSwitch and SaveStatus (`.page[data-focus-mode='true'] .editorHeader > *:not(.titleInput)` `display: none`)
     - `.sidebar`: `display: none`
   - Typewriter scroll: `.editor[data-typewriter-scroll='true']` — handled via JS in `useFocusMode` effect using `textarea.scrollTop` adjustment on `input` event (set `scroll-padding-top` and adjust scroll to keep cursor line at ~30% viewport height)
   - `.focusControlsWrap` — absolute positioning inside `.editorCanvas` for the FocusControls cluster; hide when not in focus mode
   - Responsive:
     - Desktop: centered column `max-width: 720px`
     - Tablet: full-width with generous padding
     - Mobile: `FocusControls` becomes a single compact row at top of canvas

6. **Typewriter scroll implementation** (in `useFocusMode.ts`):
   - Add a `useEffect` that attaches `input` event listener to the textarea ref (pass ref as optional param)
   - On `input`: calculate `caretOffset = textarea.selectionStart`, measure line position relative to viewport height, adjust `textarea.scrollTop` so cursor stays at ~30% from top
   - Graceful degrade: if content < viewport height, do not force scroll

## Test plan

- **Enter focus**: click "Focus" button → sidebar hides, ViewModeSwitch hides, AppShell nav visible check (only page-level chrome is hidden in v1), centered canvas appears
- **Exit focus**: "Exit focus" button in FocusControls → all UI restored; Escape key also exits
- **Typewriter scroll**: type content past viewport → cursor stays near top-third of screen; toggle off → normal scroll resumes; preference persists across page reload
- **Timer start**: click "30 min" → timer runs, shows elapsed/remaining live; title area still fully editable during timer
- **Timer complete**: wait for timer to reach 0 (or use short test duration) → summary card shows duration + words written; click Dismiss → returns to idle
- **Word count delta**: note starting word count, write 50 words, complete timer → summary shows approximately 50
- **Persistence**: reload while typewriter pref on → pref loads from localStorage
- **Keyboard**: Tab to Focus button → Enter enters mode; Tab through FocusControls → all controls reachable; Enter/Space activates each
- **No modal blocking**: timer summary is inline, never blocks typing
- Manual check: story editor textarea remains fully usable throughout all modes

## Risks and mitigations

- **AppShell global nav hiding**: in v1, only page-level chrome (sidebar, ViewModeSwitch, header controls) is hidden via CSS on `.page` root; AppShell header remains visible to avoid complex inter-component communication. If user feedback requires full chrome hide, a follow-up ticket can add a global CSS class via `document.body`
- **Typewriter scroll browser inconsistency**: test on Chrome/Safari/Firefox; add `scrollIntoView` fallback if `scrollTop` calculation is unreliable
- **Word count simplification**: whitespace-split word count is intentionally simple for v1; matches common writer tool conventions

## Rollback strategy
All code is additive. Removing the "Focus" button from `.editorHeader` in `page.tsx` fully hides the feature. The `data-focus-mode` attribute has no effect when never set to `'true'`.

## Observability
- `console.log('FocusMode: entered/exited')` on mode transitions
- `console.log('SessionTimer: completed', { duration, wordsWritten })` on timer completion

## Acceptance mapping
- "Writer can enter and exit Focus Mode from the story editor with one action" → Focus button + Escape key
- "Non-essential panels are hidden and editor remains fully usable" → CSS `data-focus-mode` hides sidebar, ViewModeSwitch, header controls
- "Typewriter-scroll can be toggled on/off and persists across refresh" → `useFocusMode` persists to localStorage
- "Session timer can start from preset durations and shows elapsed/remaining time" → `useSessionTimer` + `FocusControls` timer section
- "Exiting a completed timer session shows a summary with words written" → inline summary card in `FocusControls`

## Handoff

Assign to: Code Monkey


</plan>

title: Create shareable read-only chapter preview links for private feedback
description:
## Problem Writers need lightweight external feedback before publishing, but the app currently lacks a safe, low-friction way to share draft chapters without granting full edit access. ## Target user Solo writers seeking feedback from trusted readers/critique partners during drafting and revision. ## Outcome / KPI - Increase number of stories that receive at least one external feedback cycle - Reduce time from draft completion to first feedback received - Improve retention of writers in revision stages ## Scope - Add per-chapter `Share preview` action that creates a read-only link - Allow writer to set optional expiration date and regenerate/revoke links - Preview page shows chapter content only (no editor controls, no internal workspace) - Capture lightweight reader reactions (thumbs up/down + short comment) - Surface feedback entries back in writer’s chapter sidebar ## Non-goals - Full public publishing/discovery feed - Real-time collaborative editing permissions - Advanced moderation/community management systems ## Acceptance criteria - Writer can generate, copy, and revoke a preview link per chapter - Link opens a read-only page accessible without app account - Expired/revoked links no longer expose chapter content - Reader feedback submitted through preview appears in writer view within the chapter context - Sharing controls are clearly scoped to chapter-level content only ## Priority rationale This introduces an Inkitt-inspired feedback loop in a controlled, private form that fits MVP progression and helps writers iterate faster without expanding into full community infrastructure. ## Handoff Assign to: Market Research ## Goal Add per-story "Share Preview" that creates a read-only link with optional expiry and revocation. The preview page shows story content to anonymous readers who can submit a thumbs-up/down reaction and optional comment. Feedback surfaces in the writer's sidebar under a new "Feedback" tab. > **v1 architecture note:** The app is entirely localStorage-based (no backend). Preview link tokens and their content snapshots are persisted in localStorage. The /preview/[token] page reads from the same browser's localStorage. This means sharing across devices requires a future backend upgrade; for v1 this is a same-browser prototype and is explicitly in-scope as MVP. --- ## Scope and files ### New files | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `src/types/preview.ts` | PreviewLink and PreviewFeedback types | | `src/lib/previewLinks.ts` | localStorage CRUD for links and feedback | | `src/app/preview/[token]/page.tsx` | Anonymous reader page | | `src/app/preview/[token]/page.module.css` | Reader page styles | | `src/components/SharePreviewDialog/SharePreviewDialog.tsx` | Share dialog (generate/copy/revoke/expire) | | `src/components/SharePreviewDialog/SharePreviewDialog.module.css` | Dialog styles | | `src/components/PreviewFeedbackPanel/PreviewFeedbackPanel.tsx` | Sidebar feedback tab content | | `src/components/PreviewFeedbackPanel/PreviewFeedbackPanel.module.css` | Panel styles | ### Modified files | File | Change | |------|--------| | `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` | Add "Feedback" sidebar tab; add "Share Preview" button in editor header | | `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` | Add .sharePreviewBtn style | --- ## Step-by-step plan ### Step 1 — Define types (`src/types/preview.ts`) ```ts export type PreviewLinkStatus = 'active' | 'expired' | 'revoked'; export interface PreviewLink { token: string; // 32-char URL-safe string storyId: string; storyTitle: string; contentSnapshot: string; // snapshot of autosaved content at link creation time createdAt: number; expiresAt: number | null; // null = no expiry status: PreviewLinkStatus; } export interface PreviewFeedback { id: string; token: string; storyId: string; reaction: 'up' | 'down'; comment: string; // max 500 chars, may be empty string readerId: string; // e.g. "Reader 1", from per-token sequence submittedAt: number; isRead: boolean; // for unread badge } ``` ### Step 2 — Implement `src/lib/previewLinks.ts` Storage keys: - `preview_links_{storyId}` → `PreviewLink[]` - `preview_feedback_{storyId}` → `PreviewFeedback[]` - `preview_reader_seq_{token}` → number (incrementing reader ID counter) Functions to implement: 1. `generateToken()`: `crypto.randomUUID().replace(/-/g,'').slice(0,32)` 2. `createPreviewLink(storyId, storyTitle, content, expiresAt?)` → PreviewLink - Enforce max 3 active links (count active links; throw descriptive error if >= 3) - Status starts as 'active'; log `Created preview link ${token} for story ${storyId}` 3. `getPreviewLinks(storyId)` → PreviewLink[] (all, newest first) 4. `getActivePreviewLinks(storyId)` → PreviewLink[] (status active + not yet expired) 5. `resolvePreviewLink(token)` → PreviewLink | null - Scan all `preview_links_*` keys in localStorage to find by token - If found and expiresAt is past: auto-update status to 'expired', persist, return null - Return null if not found, expired, or revoked 6. `revokePreviewLink(storyId, token)` → void — set status to 'revoked', persist 7. `regeneratePreviewLink(storyId, oldToken, content)` → PreviewLink - Revoke old link, create new one with same expiresAt 8. `submitFeedback(token, storyId, reaction, comment)` → void - Increment reader counter for token in localStorage - Assign readerId as "Reader N" - Persist to `preview_feedback_{storyId}` 9. `getFeedback(storyId)` → PreviewFeedback[] sorted newest first 10. `markFeedbackRead(storyId, feedbackIds)` → void (set isRead=true for given ids) 11. `getUnreadFeedbackCount(storyId)` → number All functions guard with `typeof window === 'undefined'` check before localStorage access. ### Step 3 — Build `SharePreviewDialog` component Props: `{ storyId: string, storyTitle: string, content: string, onClose: () => void }` State: `links: PreviewLink[]`, `confirmRevokeToken: string | null`, `copySuccessToken: string | null`, `expiryInput: string`, `isCreating: boolean` Behaviour: - On mount: load active links via `getActivePreviewLinks(storyId)` - **Link list** (when links exist): each row shows: - Status badge: Active / Expired / Revoked (grayscale pill with `.statusBadge`) - Created date (relative via existing `relativeTime` lib) - Expiry date or "No expiry" - **Copy button**: copies `window.location.origin + '/preview/' + token`; sets `copySuccessToken` for 2s; aria-live region shows "Copied!" - **Regenerate button**: calls `regeneratePreviewLink`, refreshes list - **Revoke button**: sets `confirmRevokeToken`; shows inline "Revoke this link? Confirm / Cancel"; on Confirm calls `revokePreviewLink`, refreshes list - **Create link section** (active count < 3): - Date input (type="date") for optional expiry - "Create preview link" button: calls `createPreviewLink`, auto-copies new link, refreshes list - **Max links notice** (active count >= 3): "Maximum 3 active preview links reached. Revoke one to create a new link." - **Reaction checkpoints toggle**: `` labelled "Enable structured checkpoints (coming soon)" - Dialog structure: `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, `aria-labelledby="share-dialog-title"`, focus trap (follow existing ConfirmDialog pattern) - Close button (×) and Escape key call `onClose` Styles (`SharePreviewDialog.module.css`): - `.overlay`: `position: fixed; inset: 0; background: rgba(26,26,26,0.6); z-index: 100` - `.dialog`: `position: fixed; top: 50%; left: 50%; transform: translate(-50%,-50%); background: var(--color-white); padding: var(--spacing-xl); max-width: 480px; width: 90vw; border-radius: 8px; z-index: 101` - `.statusBadge`: `font-size: var(--font-size-xs); padding: 2px 6px; border-radius: 4px; border: 1px solid`; active = `--color-elephant-500`, expired = `--color-fashion-300`, revoked = `--color-cement-400` + `text-decoration: line-through` - `.copySuccess`: `font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--color-cement-400); font-style: italic` ### Step 4 — Build `PreviewFeedbackPanel` component Props: `{ storyId: string }` Behaviour: - On mount: call `getFeedback(storyId)`, call `markFeedbackRead(storyId, allIds)` after loading - Re-load on focus (use `useEffect` with no dependency guard — simple refresh on mount is sufficient for v1) - Empty states: - No active links + no feedback: "Share a chapter for private feedback. Create your first preview link." - Has active links, no feedback yet: "Waiting for reader feedback. Share this link with trusted readers." - Feedback list: each row shows `readerId`, reaction as text ("Liked" / "Disliked"), comment text (if non-empty), relative date Styles (`PreviewFeedbackPanel.module.css`): - `.panel`: full height, overflow-y auto (match CollabPanel structure) - `.feedbackRow`: `padding: var(--spacing-md); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--color-fog-200)` - `.readerLabel`: `font-weight: 600; font-size: var(--font-size-sm)` - `.reactionLabel`: `font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--color-cement-400)` - `.comment`: `font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-elephant-500); margin-top: var(--spacing-xs)` - `.emptyState`: `padding: var(--spacing-xl); text-align: center; color: var(--color-cement-400); font-size: var(--font-size-sm)` ### Step 5 — Build reader page (`src/app/preview/[token]/page.tsx`) Structure mirrors the story page pattern: - `'use client'`; inner component receives token from `use(params)` (Next.js 15 async params) - On mount: call `resolvePreviewLink(token)` - State: `link: PreviewLink | null`, `loading: boolean`, `reaction: 'up' | 'down' | null`, `comment: string`, `submitted: boolean` **Error path** (null link): render clean error card — "This preview is no longer available." — no content exposure **Valid path**: - Minimal header: "Story Writing App" (static string) + small "Preview" label - Story title as `

` - Content in `
` with `white-space: pre-wrap` (no textarea, no editor imports) - Feedback form: - "Leave a reaction" heading - Two `

title: Add POV and character-presence balance dashboard for draft diagnostics
description:
## Problem
Writers often discover late that POV distribution and character presence are unintentionally imbalanced, causing pacing and perspective issues that require costly rewrite passes. Current scene metadata helps capture context but does not provide manuscript-level balance diagnostics.

## Target user
Multi-POV and ensemble-cast fiction writers who need quick visibility into perspective and presence distribution during drafting/revision.

## Outcome / KPI
- Reduce late-stage rewrites caused by accidental POV/character imbalance
- Increase usage of scene metadata during active drafting
- Increase completion rate of planned revision passes

## Scope
- Add a diagnostics dashboard summarizing POV distribution across chapters/scenes
- Add character-presence heatmap by chapter with filter by major/minor characters
- Flag configurable imbalance thresholds (e.g., POV dominance, long character absence)
- Provide deep links from each diagnostic signal to the relevant scenes
- Allow writers to dismiss or snooze specific warnings per manuscript

## Non-goals
- Auto-rewriting scenes to fix imbalance
- Prescriptive “correct” narrative formulas
- Sentiment/style scoring in this ticket

## Acceptance criteria
- Dashboard renders POV share and character-presence views for a manuscript with scene metadata
- Writer can click any flagged item and navigate to impacted scenes
- Threshold warnings are deterministic and documented in-product
- Writer can dismiss/snooze warnings and settings persist per story
- Dashboard remains usable on manuscripts with at least 100 scenes

## Priority rationale
This is a high-value revision aid that extends existing metadata work into actionable insight, helping writers produce stronger drafts faster and differentiating beyond basic structure tools.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research
